headline: Meta’s AI Woes Aren’t a Unique Problem Within the Magnificent Seven
summary: Artificial intelligence has been a persistent sticking point for  Meta Platforms  but its problems compared with the other Magnificent Seven stocks have been greatly exaggerated.  Meta stock tumbled 3.8% on Friday on a report that the Facebook and Instagram parent was delaying the release of its upcoming AI model after it failed to perform as well as rivals.  Meta may even consider temporarily licensing Google’s Gemini for use in its products, the  New York Times  reported, citing unnamed sources.

summary: -link data-ticker=GOOGL data-exchange=NASDAQ href=https://www.benzinga.com/quote/GOOGL target=_blank rel=noopener>(NASDAQ: GOOGL), One week after we celebrated the International Women's day, here is a stark reminder how the C-suite remains underrepresented by women.Data from a leadership-focused ETF underscores how uncommon female leadership remains among large U.S. companies. The Wedbush ReturnOnLeadership U.S. Large-Cap ETF (ARCA: EXEQ) universe of 50 companies, only about 10% are led by women CEOs. Those include Jayshree Ullal of Arista Networks Inc (NYSE: ANET), Lori Koch of DuPont de Nemours Inc (NYSE: DD), Martina Cheung of S&P Global Inc (NYSE: SPGI), Ashley McEvoy of Insulet Corp (NASDAQ: PODD) and Debra Cafaro of Ventas Inc (NYSE: VTR). The ETF tracks companies ranked through a leadership execution framework developed by Indiggo. Janeen Gelbart, co-founder and CEO of the firm, said the methodology evaluates corporate leadership broadly rather than focusing on individual executives.

headline: Commerce Dept. quietly withdraws proposed rule on global AI chip exports
summary: Investing.com — The U.S. Department of Commerce has abruptly withdrawn a planned rule aimed at tightening global exports of advanced artificial intelligence chips, according to a government website posting on Friday. The draft regulation, which had been under interagency review since late February, was widely expected to serve as a cornerstone in the administration’s strategy to govern the international distribution of high-end semiconductor technology.
